item.title: How to loop over a Lua array/table in C/C++
item.description: Took me a while to figure it out and a lot of non-helpful Googling but looping over a table from Lua using the C API turns out to be pretty simple although not intuitive at all.

item.title: Fast HTTP body searching in JavaScript to parse strings (Part 1)
item.description: Searching strings in JavaScript is easy right? what about searching through hundreds of megabytes of text or binary or even several gigabytes of POSTed data? Boyer-Moore Horspool to the rescue!

item.title: I'm looking for a job. Part 2, Humans are hard and individuality
item.description: A follow up to my previous post on the human side of why I'm looking for rejuvination in the workforce.

item.title: I'm looking for a job. Part 1, Why?
item.description: So, after 5 years of relative success as a freelancer I'm looking for a role in a company I can add value to. But why?

item.title: Being a mobile developer in 2018 is amazingly easy, here's why.
item.description: Dave Mackintosh has developed websites, apps and libraries on mobile since 2012. here's a list of the reasons I can think of why its amazing and sometimes career saving.

item.title: Widi, a small project with a giant footprint with Babel.
